evidence.text: Time off policy is pretty good. Horrible benefits

Very laid-back. Management usually has your back. Annual, merit, and promotional raises. 401k. Medical, dental, vision, family leave, workman's comp, share matching. Opportunities to move into higher positions if you put in the work. Laid-back culture, good pay, good benefits. Great for someone entry level
Obviously the pay and benefits are pros. Especially when they tell you about the bonus and commissions- thatâs a lie. Other than the benefits there not much else. Single worst working environment
Many great colleagues and management was pretty strong. Good Culture - Less-than-good Pay
Time off is okay. Okay insurance Run away
great work life balance and flexible work hours Work life balance
Good work life balance and friendly co-workers Good people and work life balance
Work life balance is good. Work life balance

evidence.role: Current employee - Project Finance Senior Analyst
evidence.text: Knowledgeable team, excellent work-life balance, tons of hands on experience Excellent Work-Life Balance for Finance

Nice coworkers, good work life balance. This company is great for mid tier employees who like to get their job done and go home. Pay is standard, nothing exceptional. Projects move slow but can be expected. Good work life balance
Great place to start career Career growth
Typical engineering role, benefits are okay Career growth lacking

evidence.role: Current employee - Junior cloud security engineer
evidence.text: Great people and mentors Many opportunities for growth Perfect early career opportunity Great Opportunities for Growth
Great job. Poor growth opportunities. Great job. Poor growth
Learned a lot of IT things. Great to work for, a lot of professional growth.
Employee well-being is a core focus of the company's values and practices. The company is committed to fostering a supportive and inclusive work environment

Great work environment. All of my coworkers were kind and welcoming. Fairly slow paced work environment with a good work-life balance which left me feeling more relaxed rather than stressed after a day/week of work. Overall solid place to work
Solid support structure from tech support supervisor and managers. Great support

Work Environment â¢ Collaborative and supportive team culture. â¢ A diverse and inclusive workplace that values employee contributions. â¢ Modern and well-equipped facilities to foster productivity. Unpredictable company with too many changes
Global market leader in industrial automation No career advancement
Great financial results driven company Loyalty
Opportunities to grow (although it takes a long time in a company of this size). Job security. Easy to move within the company to find job that fits best. Great culture
Over time Looks good on your resume Toxic environment
Good coworkers with strong knowledge of their field and equipment they work on Good experience, strong job security
Flexible work and great co-workers. Decent job security. SQM at Siemens
Not as cool as aerospace. No Parking. OK working environment. Need more engineers. Perpetually understaffed Stable, good pay / benefits, room to advance, not too exciting but not too boring either

$78 Different company now from when I started. Not a good place anymore for career growth

Pay is less, career growth is not addressed Good
Hard work not rewarded, more taken advantage of. Pay and growth based on favoritism. HR/P&O basically nonexistent. Not a people first environment
same thing everyday since its a manufacturing plant Great for chill but not to learn or advancement
Compensation Is not pretty good Good work life balance and employees friendly
Hard to get promoted Salary below average industry wide Stable giant
Terrible management. Terrible work-life balance. no future goals for hard working employees. No pay increase. Unclear expectations. I cannot recommend
Lot of travel Low pay Not Great
poor pay and no work life balance poor pay
Some of the internal groups do not work well together Great place to work

Like most company's of this side, teams can be very siloed and there can be a lot of internal competition over the same prospects. However, overall, it seems like things are continually getting better in that regard. Awesome technology and great culture

evidence.role: Former employee - Fire and Life Safety Specialist
evidence.text: Not much connection between corporate office and field sales. Teams were disconnected and did not work well together. Management leave you to figure it out on your own. Good Company
Unrealistic timelines. Poor communication between teams. Electrical Engineer
Working in a hostile environment, and working with certain union journeymen you can be racially profiled. Blue collar work
Learn a lot in the first few years but can't move up the ladder without being a manager. Stable Job

Toxic environment. People took you for granted and treated you like you were their guinea pig Toxic Enviroment
No Strategic vision, no plan just panic and chair ahuffling Toxic

Stay far away from this company. Upper management at Siemens Government Technologies is completely self-serving, with no leadership, communication, or respect for their employees. The culture is toxicâdriven by insecurity, overwork, and zero training or support from those at the top. Terrible Management
